# Salesforce OMS - Full Documentation

# Anypoint Partner Manager Setup Guide

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

To configure Anypoint Partner Manager, follow the Postman collection instructions in the
[Accelerators B2B Common Resources](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/accel-b2b-common-resources-src/)
project.

The collection creates the following configurations:

- Endpoints, message types, and custom message attributes
- Partners
- Message flows

Once the collection runs successfully, go to Partner Manager in Anypoint Platform and verify that
the configurations were created successfully.

## Run the Postman Collection

Use the Partner Manager setup collection in the
[Accelerator B2B Common Resource](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/accel-b2b-common-resources-src/)
project to configure message flows and their components in Partner Manager for the Accelerator for
Salesforce OMS solution.

To run the collection, you must set the following variables:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Property Name</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>env_identifier_x12-isa-zz_id</p></td><td><p>The ID of the identifier type X12-ISA-ZZ.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>env_identifier_x12-gs_id</p></td><td><p>The ID of the identifier type X12-GS for partner.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>env_identifier_ref-id_id</p></td><td><p>The ID of the identifier type Reference ID. This is static across all the organizations.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>http_endpoint_type_id</p></td><td><p>The <code>endpointTypeId</code> of an HTTPS endpoint type. The value is the same across all environments. Note that you can find the <code>endpointTypeId</code> value by opening the details of an endpoint in Partner Manager, and then running a request from the <strong>Network</strong> tab in the developer view of your browser. The value is available from the <strong>Preview</strong> tab of the run result.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>host_anypoint_org</p></td><td><p>The host name of the Anypoint Platform organization.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>devx.orgid</p></td><td><p>The ID of the Anypoint Platform organization.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>devx.envid</p></td><td><p>The ID of the environment in the Anypoint Platform organization.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>partner_x12_isa_identifier_value</p></td><td><p>The value of the partner identifier type X12-ISA-ZZ. This example uses <code>ORANGE</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>partner_reference_id_identifier_value</p></td><td><p>The value of the partner identifier type Reference ID. This example uses <code>ORANGE</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>partner_x12_gs_identifier_type_value</p></td><td><p>The value of the partner identifier type X12 - GS. This example uses <code>ORANGE</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>host_x12_isa_identifier_value</p></td><td><p>The value of the host identifier type X12-ISA-ZZ. This example uses <code>MYTHICAL</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>host_reference_id_identifier_value</p></td><td><p>The value of the host identifier type Reference ID. This example uses <code>MYTHICAL</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>host_x12_gs_identifier_type_value</p></td><td><p>The value of the host identifier type X12 - GS. This example uses <code>MYTHICAL</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>b2b_orders_process_api_host_name</p></td><td><p>The host name of the B2B Orders Process API.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>b2b_orders_prc_api_orders_path</p></td><td><p>The URI path for <code>Orders</code> in the B2B Orders Process API.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>b2b_storage_sys_api_host_name</p></td><td><p>The host name of the B2B Storage System API.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>b2b_erp_prc_api_host_name</p></td><td><p>The host name of the ERP Process API. This example uses the B2B Simulator Process API.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>b2b_erp_prc_api_invoices_path</p></td><td><p>The URI path for Invoices in the ERP Process API. This example uses the B2B Simulator Process API.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>b2b_orders_prc_api_payments_path</p></td><td><p>The URI path for Payments in the B2B Orders Process API.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>b2b_erp_prc_api_acknowledgements_path</p></td><td><p>The URI path for Acknowledgments in the ERP Process API. This example uses the B2B Simulator Process API.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>partner_name</p></td><td><p>The name of the partner.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>partner_description</p></td><td><p>The description of the partner.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>partner_website_url</p></td><td><p>The website URL of the partner.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Prerequisites](prerequisites)
- [MuleSoft Accelerator for Salesforce OMS](./)
---

# Anypoint MQ Setup Guide

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

To create the Anypoint MQ destinations that are required by the MuleSoft Accelerator for Salesforce
OMS components, use the `Anypoint-MQ-Configuration` Postman collection in the
[Accelerator B2B Common Resources](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/accel-b2b-common-resources-src/)
project.

## Environment Settings

Create the `dev` environment by using the template in the
[Accelerator B2B Common Resources](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/accel-b2b-common-resources-src/)
project and importing it into your Postman workspace. Assign the values that reflect your target
Anypoint Platform org before running the requests.

## Run the Collection

The collection defines the variables that you must override prior to running requests. The
collection is organized into separate folders, which you must execute in the following order:

1.  Authorization
2.  Client-Management - This variable is optional.
3.  Queue-Management
4.  Exchange-Management

Run requests in the Verification folder to validate the results of the previous operations. You can
also run the requests in the Deletions folder to clean up objects created by the collection.

## Verify Destinations

Successful execution of the collection creates the following Anypoint MQ destinations:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Queue Name</th><th>Bound to Exchange</th><th>Dead Letter Queue Name</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>accel-b2b-orders-update-queue</p></td><td><p>accel-oms-update-exchange</p></td><td><p>accel-b2b-orders-update-dl-queue</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>accel-oms-exception-queue</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>accel-retail-update-queue</p></td><td><p>accel-retail-update-exchange</p></td><td><p>accel-retail-exception-dl-queue</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>accel-shipping-update-queue</p></td><td><p>accel-shipping-update-exchange</p></td><td><p>accel-shipping-exception-dl-queue</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Prerequisites](prerequisites)
- [MuleSoft Accelerator for Salesforce OMS](./)
---

# MuleSoft Accelerator for Salesforce OMS

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The MuleSoft Accelerator for Salesforce OMS enables you to integrate your Order Management System
(OMS) with external business partners and application ecosystems by leveraging the application and
Business-to-Business (B2B) integration capabilities of Anypoint Platform. The solution accelerates
the implementation of Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) messaging and other integrations into
Salesforce OMS.

## Use Cases

### B2B EDI Order Lifecycle Automation

The
[B2B EDI Order Lifecycle Automation](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-salesforce-oms/minor/1.1/pages/7ij-1pd/B2B%20EDI%20Order%20Lifecycle%20Automation/)
use case lets you add EDI as a direct order intake channel to manage and service EDI orders across
the full order management lifecycle.

### Salesforce OMS to ERP Integration

Manage orders received via omnichannel sources within Salesforce Order Management System (OMS) with
the
[Salesforce OMS to ERP Integration](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-salesforce-oms/minor/1.1/pages/e74-ta9/Salesforce%20OMS%20to%20ERP%20Integration/)
use case.

## See Also

- [Getting Started](../../accelerators-home/getting-started)
- [Prerequisites](prerequisites)
- [Release Notes](../../release-notes/accelerators/salesforce-oms/salesforce-oms-release-notes)
- [MuleSoft Accelerator for Salesforce OMS on Exchange](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-salesforce-oms/)
---

# SAP S/4HANA Order Management Guide

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## Overview

This guide helps you create a sales order, freight order, outbound delivery, and invoice in SAP so
that you can implement the
[Salesforce OMS to ERP Integration](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-salesforce-oms/minor/1.1/pages/e74-ta9/Salesforce%20OMS%20to%20ERP%20Integration/)
use case.

## Before You Begin

Submit a ticket on [SAP for Me](https://me.sap.com/) to request activation of the following scope
items in your Central Business Configuration (CBC) provisioned S/4HANA Cloud Essentials tenant:

- Manual Transportation Planning (6W2)
- Transportation Execution (6W3)
- Freight Settlement (6W4)

For more information about how to complete the ticket, see the
[Scope item activation request for CBC provisioned S/4HANA Cloud Essentials tenant](https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3122430)
knowledge base article on the SAP support site.

### Generated SAP Documents

Completing the steps in this guide generates the following documents:

- **Sales Order**

  A contractual agreement between a sales organization and a sold-to party to deliver products or
  provide a service at a set price, quantity, and time.

- **Freight Order**

  The order that a carrier or shipper plans to execute, containing the logistical processing plan,
  vehicle departure times, and order execution data.

- **Outbound Delivery Order**

  Contains the data that is required to trigger and monitor the outbound delivery process.

- **Billing Document**

  The invoice that is created for an outbound delivery and requests payment from the customer who
  placed the order.

## Create a Sales Order

Follow these steps to create a sales order in SAP:

1.  Search for the **Create Sales Order - VA01** app.
2.  Enter **OR** in the **Order Type** field.
3.  Press Enter.
4.  Enter the customer identification number in the **Sold-to Party** field.
5.  Enter the customer identification number in the **Ship-to Party** field.
6.  Click the **Display header details** icon (![3%](_images/display-header-details-icon.png)) and
    verify that the **Shp. Cond** on the **Shipping** tab is set to **TM - Int. Planning**, which
    enables SAP to create a freight unit automatically. The freight unit is used when the freight
    order is created.
7.  Enter the product identification number that you are including in the sales order in the
    **Material** column of the **All Items** section.
8.  Enter a value in the **Order Quantity** field.
9.  Press Enter.
10. Click **Save**.

Copy the sales order ID, which youâll need in the next steps.

## Create a Freight Order

Follow these steps to create a freight order in SAP:

1.  Search for the **Transportation Cockpit** app.
2.  Select **ERP Document** and paste the sales order ID into the **Freight Unit Stages** field.
3.  Enter an asterisk in the **Vehicle Types** field.
4.  Click **Continue**.
5.  Select the freight unit of the sales order in the **Freight Unit** column.
6.  Select the truck type in the **Trucks** field.
7.  Click **Create Road Freight Order** in the **Freight Unit Stages** field and then click **Save**
    to create the freight order.
8.  Click the freight order number to open the order details and then complete the **Vehicle Type**,
    **Carrier**, **Purchasing Organization**, and **Purchasing Group** fields.
9.  Enter an amount in the **Base FTL - Flat Rate** field on the **Charges** tab and then click
    **Save**.
10. Select **Send to Carrier** from the **Subcontracting** dropdown on the **Statuses** tab.
11. Then, select **Set to Confirmed by Carrier** from the same **Subcontracting** dropdown.
12. Select **Custom Fields** from the **All Tabs** icon (![3%](_images/all-tabs-icon.png)) and
    complete the **Tracking Number** field.
13. Click **Save**.

## Create an Outbound Delivery

Follow these steps to create an outbound delivery in SAP:

1.  Click the **Create Deliveries** tab and then click the **Document Flow** tab.
2.  Select the box for each document that is related to the freight order.
3.  Open SAP S/4HANA in a new tab and search for the **Change Outbound Delivery** app.
4.  Enter the outbound delivery number from the **Create Deliveries** tab in the **Outbound
    Delivery** field and then press Enter.
5.  On the **Picking** tab, enter the same value in the **Picked Qty** column thatâs in the **Deliv.
    Qty** column to update the status in the **OvrllPickStatus** and **Warehouse Execution** fields.
6.  Select **Post Goods Issue** from the **Menu** dropdown to trigger the
    `Outbound Delivery API (DELIVERY_REQUEST_OUT)` event.
7.  Return to the freight order and click **Refresh** to update the status fields on the
    **Statuses** tab.
8.  Click the **Edit** button.
9.  Select **Set to Ready for Transportation Execution** in the **Execution Status** dropdown to
    update the value in the **Execution Status** field to **Ready for Transportation Execution**.
10. Select **Set to Departed** in the **Execution Status (Source)** dropdown in the **Stages**
    section.

## Create an Invoice

Follow these steps to create an invoice in SAP:

1.  Search for the **Create Billing Documents - VF01** app.
2.  Enter the outbound delivery number in the **Document** column of the **Documents to Be
    Processed** table and then press Enter.
3.  Click **Save**.

## View Your Documents

Follow these steps in SAP to view the documents you created:

1.  Search for the **Manage Sales Order Version 2** app and then filter by the sales order ID.
2.  Click the sales order ID in the **Sales Orders** table to open the order details.
3.  Click **Process Flow** to view the documents you created.

## See Also

- [Prerequisites](prerequisites)
---

# Prerequisites for MuleSoft Accelerator for OMS

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The amount of configuration youâll need to perform depends on which assets youâre deploying in your
target environment.

Use the following setup guides to configure the systems and products that are needed to implement
the
[B2B EDI Order Lifecycle Automation](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-salesforce-oms/minor/1.1/pages/7ij-1pd/B2B%20EDI%20Order%20Lifecycle%20Automation/)
use case:

- [Anypoint MQ Setup](anypointmq-setup-guide)
- [Salesforce OMS Setup and Customization](sfdc-oms-setup-customization-guide)
- [Anypoint Partner Manager Setup](anypoint-partner-manager-setup-guide)

Use the following setup guides to configure the systems and products that are needed to implement
the
[Salesforce OMS to ERP Integration](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-salesforce-oms/minor/1.1/pages/e74-ta9/Salesforce%20OMS%20to%20ERP%20Integration/)
use case:

- [Anypoint MQ Setup](anypointmq-setup-guide)
- [SAP S/4HANA Order Management](order-mgmt-guide)
- [SAP S/4HANA Setup](sap-s4hana-setup-guide)

## See Also

- [Getting Started](../../accelerators-home/getting-started)
- [MuleSoft Accelerator for Salesforce OMS](./)
---

# SAP S4HANA Setup Guide

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## Overview

To configure and test an S/4HANA Cloud SOAP outbound service (Product replication:
`productmdmbulkreplicaterequest`) and the SAP S/4HANA Cloud SOAP Connector as a Mule application
deployed on CloudHub, see the \[SAP S/4HANA Setup Guide\] on Exchange.

Follow the steps in the [S/4HANA Customization Guide](#s4hana-customization-guide) section to set up
your Salesforce OMS to ERP Integration.

## Configure SAP S/4HANA

To make the necessary configurations in S/4HANA:

1.  Create a **Communication System** in the corresponding Fiori app. This communication system
    represents the external system that will consume the S/4HANA Cloud SOAP service. In this
    example, itâs named `COM_0009_SOAP_OUT`:
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-01.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-01.png)
2.  For the 'Business System' name, use the same name of the System ID.
3.  In the 'Host Name' field, enter the hostname of the Mule application deployed on Cloudhub (if
    you donât have it yet, you can enter a mock URL and then modify it later):
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-02.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-02.png)
4.  Create a user that will be used to connect to the Cloud Hub app for sending product information
    in the outbound scenario (this is necessary even though the Mule application does not validate
    the user; we used any user for outbound):
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-03.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-03.png)
5.  Use the **Communication Arrangement** Fiori app to create a new Communication Arrangement:
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-04.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-04.png)
6.  Create a new **Communication Arrangement**, select the communication scenario "SAP_COM_0009",
    and then define a specific name for the arrangement, "SAP_COM_0009_SOAP".
7.  In the **Communication System** field, select the Communication System created in the previous
    step:
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-05.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-05.png)
8.  For outbound communication, select the previously-created user and configure the **Product
    Master - Replicate from SAP S/4HANA Cloud to Client** and include the following settings. Make
    sure to note the Path (`/sap/bc/srt/scs/sap/productmdmbulkreplicaterequest`) because we will use
    this path to configure the Mule application SOAP endpoint:
    - Set the **Service status** to **Active**
    - Replication Model (any name)
    - Replication model = `I (Initial)`
    - The Output Mode = `D (Direct)`
      ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-06.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-06.png)
      ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-07.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-07.png)
      ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-08.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-08.png)

9.  Save it. All configurations at S/4HANA Cloud side are finished and you will be able to download
    the WSDL that contains the metadata to import into the S/4HANA Cloud SOAP Connector into the
    Mule application.

## Configurations in the Mule Application

The following example describes how to create a Mule application using the Outbound Message Listener
operation of MuleSoftâs SAP S/4HANA SOAP Connector:

1.  Create a Mule project and add the appropriate connector from Exchange to the Mule Palette:
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-09.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-09.png)
2.  Add and configure the **Outbound Message Listener** to the canvas:
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-10.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-10.png)
3.  Add the corresponding WSDL path to the WSDL you downloaded from S/4HANA Cloud and create the
    HTTP listener configuration:
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-11.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-11.png)
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-12.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-12.png)
    _Note: we are using no authentication on the Mule Application, so we used the Insecure option
    for the Trust Store Configuration. In the Keystore configuration, we used a test Keystore, which
    is mandatory for HTTPS communication:_
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-13.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-13.png)
4.  Select the Message type that the connector expects to receive from SAP (the connector reads the
    metadata from the WSDL we configured):
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-14.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-14.png)
5.  On the Responses tab, use the status Code "202" and the Reason-Phrase "Accepted" because we want
    to send the same status that S/4HANA Cloud will send if the service invocation results OK. No
    payload will be returned as a response - just the `Status` code and `Reason-phrase`:
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-15.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-15.png)
6.  Use a **Logger** component to log the inbound XML payload:
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-16.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-16.png)
    _The Mule flow should look like the following:_
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-17.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-17.png)
7.  Deploy the application to Cloudhub. Youâre now ready to test the scenario.

## Testing the Scenario

To test the scenario:

1.  Create the following. You only need to create a material in the corresponding Fiori app of
    S/4HANA Cloud:
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-18.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-18.png)
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-19.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-19.png)
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-20.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-20.png)
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-21.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-21.png)
    _After you save the new material, you should see the following confirmation in S/4HANA Cloud:_
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-22.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-22.png)
    _The following shows the XML message received at the Mule application at the Runtime Manager on
    Cloudhub:_
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-23.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-23.png)
2.  See the sent message in the Message Dashboard Fiori Application:
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-24.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-24.png)
3.  Select the date:
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-25.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-25.png)
4.  Search under **CMD:Product Integration - /CMDPR â Product Replication to Client via Service -
    PRDREQ_OUT/00001 namespace**:
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-26.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-26.png)
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-27.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-27.png)

## S/4HANA Customization Guide

Create the following custom fields in the corresponding objects in Salesforce OMS to enable the
customer, product, and orders sync use cases to function end-to-end.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Salesforce Object</th><th>Field Name</th><th>Data Type</th><th>Length</th><th>Unique</th><th>ExternalId</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>BusinessPartner</p></td><td><p>YY1_SME_MPARTYROLEID_bus</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>36</p></td><td><p>Y</p></td><td><p>Y</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>SalesOrder</p></td><td><p>YY1_SME_MORDERID_SDH</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>36</p></td><td><p>Y</p></td><td><p>Y</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Product</p></td><td><p>YY1_SME_UnvProductId_PRD</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>36</p></td><td><p>Y</p></td><td><p>Y</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Configure SAP S/4HANA Order Management

Follow the steps in these sections to configure your Salesforce OMS to ERP Integration.

#### Configure the Communication Arrangement

Use the settings in the table to configure the communication arrangements.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Communication Arrangement ID</th><th>Service Name</th><th>Protocol</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>SAP_COM_0109</p></td><td><p>Sales Order (A2X)</p></td><td><p>OData V2</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>SAP_COM_0223</p></td><td><p>Outbound Delivery - Notify (B2B)</p></td><td><p>OData V2</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>SAP_COM_0905</p></td><td><p>Transportation Management - Freight Order Integration</p></td><td><p>OData V4</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>SAP_COM_0120</p></td><td><p>Billing Document - Create with SD Document Reference</p></td><td><p>SOAP</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>SAP_COM_0120</p></td><td><p>Billing Document - Read, Cancel, GetPDF</p></td><td><p>OData V2</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Add the Required Entry for Outbound Delivery - Notify (B2B) Communication Arrangement

Use the Fiori app Output Parameter Determination to add the following entry under the rule for
**Outbound delivery** and **Determination Step Output Relevance**.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Output Type</th><th>Goods movement status</th><th>Document Category</th><th>Picking status</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>DELIVERY_NOTE</p></td><td><p>A</p></td><td><p>J</p></td><td><p>C</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

After configuring the communication arrangements, add the customer by using the rule
`Outbound Delivery` and the Determination Step Channel.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Output Type</th><th>Recipient</th><th>Channel</th><th>Rolee</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>DELIVERY_NOTE</p></td><td><p>&lt;Customer Number&gt;</p></td><td><p>EDI</p></td><td><p>WE</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Next, add the customer in the **Business Partner** section of the communication system, which is
used to configure this communication arrangement.

#### Custom CDS View

- **Name**

  YY1_SDDocumentFlow

- **Representative Key**

  SalesDocument

- **Scenario**

  External API

- **Data Sources**

  <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field name</th><th>Type</th><th>Cardinality</th><th>Join Condition</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>I_SalesDocument</p></td><td><p>Primary Datasource</p></td><td><p>N/A</p></td><td><p>N/A</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>I_SDDocumentMultiLevelProcFlow</p></td><td><p>Associated Datasource</p></td><td><p>Zero or More [0..*]</p></td><td><p>PrecedingDocument = SalesDocument</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>I_SDDocumentCategory</p></td><td><p>Associated Datasource</p></td><td><p>Zero or More [0..*]</p></td><td><p>SDDocumentCategory = SubsequentDocumentCategory</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

- **Elements**

  <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Alias</th><th>Path</th><th>Type</th><th>Key</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>SalesDocument</p></td><td><p>I_SalesDocument.SalesDocument</p></td><td><p>CHAR (10)</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>PrecedingDocumentCategory</p></td><td><p>_I_SDDocumentMultiLevelProcFlo.PrecedingDocumentCategory</p></td><td><p>CHAR (4)</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>SubsequentDocument</p></td><td><p>_I_SDDocumentMultiLevelProcFlo.SubsequentDocument</p></td><td><p>CHAR (10)</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>SubsequentDocumentCategory</p></td><td><p>_I_SDDocumentMultiLevelProcFlo.SubsequentDocumentCategory</p></td><td><p>CHAR (10)</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>ProcessFlowLevel</p></td><td><p>_I_SDDocumentMultiLevelProcFlo.ProcessFlowLevel</p></td><td><p>NUMC(2)</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>CreationDate</p></td><td><p>_I_SDDocumentMultiLevelProcFlo.CreationDate</p></td><td><p>DATS (8)</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>CreationTime</p></td><td><p>_I_SDDocumentMultiLevelProcFlo.CreationTime</p></td><td><p>TIMS (6)</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Create a Custom Communication Scenario

You must create a custom communication scenario by adding the CDS view as an inbound service.

Publish the communication scenario, and then create a communication arrangement using the custom
communication scenario you just created. The communication arrangement includes the service URL that
is used to retrieve the SD Document Flow.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>SAP Field Name</th><th>Label</th><th>Data Type</th><th>Length</th><th>SAP Business Context</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_Tracking_Nmb</p></td><td><p>Tracking Number</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>20</p></td><td><p>Transportation Orders (TM_TRANSPORTATION_ORDER)</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Prerequisites](prerequisites)
---

# Salesforce OMS Setup and Customization Guide

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

This guide describes how to configure Salesforce OMS for the
[B2B EDI Order Lifecycle Automation](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-salesforce-oms/)
use case.

## Configure Salesforce OMS

Follow the instructions in the
[Salesforce Order Management Implementation Guide for B2B and D2C Commerce](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?language=en_US&id=commerce.om_impl_intro.htm&type=5)
to configure Salesforce OMS. As part of the configuration:

- Disable the **Enable Reduction Orders** option in **Order Settings**.
- Enable **Order Management**, **Use Person Accounts**, and **High Scale Orders**
- Activate **Multiple Currencies**
- Enable **State** and **Country/Territory** picklists
- [Configure Payment Gateway](#configure_payment_gateway)
- Configure either a digital wallet or credit card payment method and associate an account with it

## Customize Salesforce OMS

Install the Salesforce package from the
[Accelerator B2B Common Resources](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/accel-b2b-common-resources-src/)
project and verify that the configuration is correct in your Salesforce organization.

Follow these steps in Salesforce OMS after installing the package successfully:

1.  Create a matching rule named `Account Interchange Code Match` in **Setup > Matching Rules**.
    Enter `Account: B2B_EDISenderCode EXACT MatchBlank = FALSE` in the **Matching Criteria** field.
2.  Create a duplicate rule named `Account Interchange Code Rule` in **Setup > Duplicate Rules**.
    Select `Account Interchange Code Match` as the matching rule. After you save and activate the
    duplicate rule, open the rule in **Duplicate Management > Duplicate Rules** and set the order to
    1 in the **Reorder** field, which gives the rule precedence over the standard **Account** object
    matching rule.

### Custom Fields

Verify that the following fields have been created in the corresponding objects:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Salesforce Object</strong></th><th><strong>Field Label</strong></th><th><strong>Field Name</strong></th><th><strong>Data Type</strong></th><th><strong>Length</strong></th><th><strong>Unique</strong></th><th><strong>ExternalId</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Account</p></td><td><p>B2B EDI Sender Code</p></td><td><p>B2B_EDISenderCode__c</p></td><td><p>Text(Case Sensitive)</p></td><td><p>15</p></td><td><p>Y</p></td><td><p>N</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account</p></td><td><p>B2B Order Currency</p></td><td><p>B2B_OrderCurrency__c</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>3</p></td><td><p>N</p></td><td><p>N</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account</p></td><td><p>B2B Order Delivery Method</p></td><td><p>B2B_OrderDeliveryMethod__c</p></td><td><p>Lookup(Order Delivery Method)</p></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>N</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account</p></td><td><p>B2B Order Sales Channel</p></td><td><p>B2B_OrderSalesChannel__c</p></td><td><p>Lookup(Sales Channel)</p></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>N</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account</p></td><td><p>B2B Order Store</p></td><td><p>B2B_OrderStore__c</p></td><td><p>Lookup(Store)</p></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>N</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Shipment</p></td><td><p>ExternalId</p></td><td><p>ExternalId__c</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>100</p></td><td><p>N</p></td><td><p>Y</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] You must add the **Read** and **Write** permissions to the custom fields created by the
> Salesforce package.

### Verify the Custom Object Tab

Verify that your Salesforce org shows the **Pending Order Summary Errors** custom tab.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Salesforce Object</strong></th><th><strong>Field Label</strong></th><th><strong>Field Name</strong></th><th><strong>Data Type</strong></th><th><strong>Length</strong></th><th><strong>Unique</strong></th><th><strong>ExternalId</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Pending Order Summary Error</p></td><td><p>Error Code</p></td><td><p>Error_Code__c</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>255</p></td><td><p>N</p></td><td><p>N</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pending Order Summary Error</p></td><td><p>Error Message</p></td><td><p>Error_Message__c</p></td><td><p>Long Text Area</p></td><td><p>5000</p></td><td><p>N</p></td><td><p>N</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pending Order Summary Error</p></td><td><p>Event Uuid</p></td><td><p>Event_Uuid__c</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>255</p></td><td><p>N</p></td><td><p>N</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pending Order Summary Error</p></td><td><p>External Reference Identifier</p></td><td><p>External_Reference_Identifier__c</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>255</p></td><td><p>N</p></td><td><p>N</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pending Order Summary Error</p></td><td><p>Pending Order Summary Error Name</p></td><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>80</p></td><td><p>N</p></td><td><p>N</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Platform Event

Verify that the `ShipmentCreated` platform event is configured exactly as shown in the following
table:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>API Name</th><th>Event Type</th><th>Publish Behavior</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>ShipmentCreated</p></td><td><p>ShipmentCreated__e</p></td><td><p>High Volume</p></td><td><p>Publish After Commit</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

In addition, verify that the following fields have been created in the `ShipmentCreated` object:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field Label</strong></th><th><strong>Field Name</strong></th><th><strong>Data Type</strong></th><th><strong>Length</strong></th><th><strong>Unique</strong></th><th><strong>ExternalId</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>OrderSummaryId</p></td><td><p>OrderSummaryId__c</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>50</p></td><td><p>N</p></td><td><p>N</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>ShipmentId</p></td><td><p>ShipmentId__c</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>50</p></td><td><p>N</p></td><td><p>N</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Flows

After installing the Salesforce package, verify that the following flows are available and
activated. Customize the flows based on your business requirements.

- `OMS_Create_OrderSummaryRoutingSchedule_on_OrderSummary_event` - Creates an Order Summary Routing
  schedule when OMS Order Summary record is created.
- `OMS_Create_Fulfillment_Orders_on_OrderSummaryRoutingSchedule` - Creates a fulfillment orders
  after an `Order Summary Routing` schedule record is created.
- `OMS_Create_Fulfillment_Orders_from_Order_Summary_Subflow` - Used in
  `OMS_Create_Fulfillment_Orders_on_OrderSummaryRoutingSchedule` flow to create fulfillment orders
  for an order summary.
- `OMS_Raise_ShipmentCreated_Event` flow - Creates a platform event when a shipment is created in
  Salesforce OMS.
- `OMS_Create_Invoice` - Creates an invoice after the fulfillment order is filled.
- `OMS_Log_Pending_Order_Summary_Error` - Creates an error record in the
  `Pending Order Summary Error` object when an error occurs during processing of the
  `Pending Order Summary Processed Event`.

### Configure Payment Gateway

If you donât use Salesforce Payments, configure a payment gateway manually to view and manage
payments in Order Management. Configure a gateway for each payment provider, including Stripe,
PayPal, Adyen, and Cybersource. You can find payment packages on AppExchange for some vendors.

Follow these steps to configure a payment gateway manually:

1.  Follow the
    [Core Payments Reference Gateway Integration Adapters](https://github.com/forcedotcom/Core-Payments-Reference-Gateway-Integration-Adapters)
    instructions to create a payment gateway adapter class that uses the Salesforce APEX connector
    framework.
2.  Create a payment gateway provider record that points to your newly created payment gateway
    adapter class.
3.  Go to **Setup** and define a named credential that contains the gateway providerâs
    authentication and login information. The adapter class callout definition calls the named
    credential.
4.  Create a payment gateway record to link the payment gateway provider record and the named
    credential.

## Error Handling in Salesforce OMS

The Salesforce High Scale Orders API initially records data for order summaries as
`PendingOrderSummary`. To troubleshoot errors that occur during the transition from
`PendingOrderSummary` to `Order Summary`, use the `OMS_Log_Pending_Order_Summary_Error.flow`
process, which subscribes to the `PendingOrderSumProcEvent` event and logs errors in the
`Pending Order Summary Error` object. The **Pending Order Summary Errors** custom tab in Salesforce
OMS shows the errors.

## Configure Your Connected App for Authentication

Follow these steps to generate the **Consumer Key** and **Consumer Secret** values required for
Salesforce authentication:

1.  In your Salesforce org, navigate to **New Connected App** in the **App Manager**.
2.  Enter "MuleSoft Accelerator" as the connected app name and API name.
3.  Enter your email address.
4.  Check the **Enable OAuth Settings**.
5.  Set the callback URL `http://localhost`.
6.  Select "Full access (full)" in the **Selected OAuth Scopes** list.
7.  Click **Save**, and then click **Continue**.
8.  Click **Manage** to view the connected app details.
9.  Click **Edit Policies**.
10. Select **All users may self-authorize** in the **OAuth Policies** section.
11. Select **Relax IP** restrictions in the **IP Relaxation** field.
12. Click **Save**.
13. Go to **Setup** and navigate to **App Manager**.
14. Select **View** from the actions for your connected app.
15. Click **Manage Consumer Details** in the **API (Enable OAuth Settings)** section.
16. Verify your identity by entering the verification code from your email.
17. Copy the values in the **Consumer Key** and **Consumer Secret** fields.
18. Add the values as the `sfdc.client-id` and `sfdc.client-secret` properties respectively in your
    Maven settings.xml file.

Use the sample settings.xml file in the Accelerator B2B Common Resources project as a starting point
for configuring your connected app.

## Generate a Security Token for Your Service Account

To connect to the Salesforce OMS Cloud instance, you must generate a token for the service account
used by Mule applications.

1.  Log in to Salesforce as the service account user, which is the account Mule applications use to
    connect to Salesforce.
2.  Click the Username icon and select **Settings**.
3.  Select **Reset My Security Token** in **My Personal Information**.
4.  Click **Reset Security Token**.

Salesfoce emails the new security token to the service account userâs address.

> [!NOTE] The option to reset your security token does not show if you have one or more login IP
> ranges. To enable the option, remove the login IP ranges from the settings page of the profile
> assigned to the service account user. Reset your security token, and then restore the login IP
> ranges.

## See Also

- [Prerequisites](prerequisites)
- [MuleSoft Accelerator for Salesforce OMS](./)
